scenario.title: The Ambiguous Code Review
scenario.context: You're a senior developer who needs AI help reviewing a junior developer's code. You want the AI to check for bugs, suggest improvements, and explain any issues it finds.
scenario.goal: Write a prompt that clearly specifies what kind of code review you want, avoiding ambiguous requests like 'review this code' or 'make it better'.
constraints: Your prompt must specify at least 3 specific things to check for
Include the level of detail expected in the feedback
Specify the format of the response you want
scenario.exampleInput: Code to review: A Python function that fetches user data from an API and caches it locally.
hints: What specific aspects should the AI focus on? Security? Performance? Readability?
How detailed should the explanations be? Junior-friendly or expert-level?
What format would make the feedback most actionable? A list? Inline comments?

rubric.name: Specificity
rubric.description: Does the prompt specify exactly what to look for?

scoringGuide.excellent: Lists 3+ specific review criteria with clear definitions
scoringGuide.good: Lists review criteria but some are vague
scoringGuide.adequate: Mentions general areas but lacks specific criteria

rubric.name: Detail Level
rubric.description: Does the prompt specify the depth of analysis expected?

scoringGuide.excellent: Explicitly states expected detail level and audience
scoringGuide.poor: No guidance on how detailed feedback should be

rubric.name: Output Structure
rubric.description: Does the prompt specify how feedback should be organized?

rubric.name: Actionability
rubric.description: Would the response be immediately useful?

scoringGuide.excellent: Request is designed to produce actionable feedback

evaluatorContext.idealResponse: Review this Python function for: 1) Security vulnerabilities (SQL injection, data exposure, input validation), 2) Performance issues (unnecessary API calls, caching efficiency, memory usage), 3) Code readability (naming conventions, documentation, complexity). For each issue found, provide: a) Severity level (critical/medium/low), b) Line number or code section, c) Explanation suitable for a junior developer, d) Suggested fix with code example. Format: Group findings by category, list critical issues first.
commonMistakes: Saying 'review this code' without specifying what to look for
Not specifying the expertise level of the intended audience
Not indicating priority or severity expectations
antiPatterns: No mention of what aspects to focus on
